# Witch Class Feats — Levels 12–20

> **Source:** Archives of Nethys (2e.aonprd.com) — Pathfinder 2nd Edition (Remastered)
> **Total Feats:** 15 (Levels 12–20)
> **Notes:** Includes all Witch-tagged feats and shared feats available to Witches. Non–Player Core sources are noted. Legacy feats (pre-remaster) are marked. Uses remastered terminology (e.g., "rank" instead of "level" for spells, "Spellshape" instead of "Metamagic" for remastered feats).

---

## Level 12 Feats (4)

---

### Coven Spell :r:

**Feat 12** | **Spellshape, Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 189

**Trigger** An ally within 30 feet Casts a Spell.

You link your magic to an ally's spell to empower it at a critical moment. Choose one of the following effects to add to the ally's spell:

- The spell gains a status bonus to damage equal to the spell's rank.
- You apply one of your spellshape feats to the ally's spell, provided the spell is a valid target for the spellshape. You must still expend any costs of the spellshape (such as spending a Focus Point), and if you can't, the spellshape has no effect.

---

### Familiar's Eyes :a:

**Feat 12** | **Concentrate, Divination, Witch** | ⚠️ *Legacy*

**Source** Advanced Player's Guide pg. 104

**Prerequisites** a familiar

You can see through the eyes of your familiar to gain its perspective. You project your senses into your familiar; for the duration you lose all sensory information from your own body, but can sense through your familiar's senses using your own Perception. You can Dismiss this effect.

While you are using your familiar's senses, you can speak through the familiar and you can communicate with it telepathically.

**Special** This feat gains the trait matching your spell tradition.

---

### Hex Focus

**Feat 12** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 190

Your connection to your patron is so strong that your hexes recover more energy. Whenever you Refocus, you regain all of your Focus Points, not just one.

---

### Witch's Broom

**Feat 12** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 190

You anoint a broom, staff, or polearm to serve as a vehicle during your daily preparations. While astride the anointed item, you have a fly Speed of 20 feet. You take a –10-foot penalty to your fly Speed if you're encumbered and carrying more than 20 Bulk, and you fall if you're carrying more than 30 Bulk.

You can Craft your anointed item into a *flying broomstick* as if you had the formula, using your witch spell DC for the Craft check. If the anointed item is already a *flying broomstick*, you instead gain a +10-foot status bonus to its fly Speed.

---

## Level 14 Feats (3)

---

### Patron's Presence

**Feat 14** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 190

Your patron exerts its presence through your familiar, unsettling your enemies. Your familiar gains the following activity.

**Patron's Presence** :aa: (aura, concentrate)

**Frequency** once per hour

Your familiar creates a 15-foot emanation. Each enemy within the emanation must succeed at a Will save against your spell DC or become stupefied 2 (stupefied 3 on a critical failure). The effect lasts until the end of the enemy's next turn. Your familiar can Sustain the aura for up to 1 minute.

---

### Reflect Spell

**Feat 14** | **Witch, Wizard** *(Shared)*

**Source** Player Core pg. 190

**Prerequisites** Counterspell

When you successfully use Counterspell to counteract a spell, you can turn it back on its caster. You send the spell back at the caster, who becomes the new target for the spell or the center of the spell's area, using the same spell DC and spell attack modifier. The reflected spell affects only the original caster, even if it is an area spell.

The spell's caster can attempt a save and use other defenses against the reflected spell as normal.

---

### Rites of Transfiguration

**Feat 14** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 190

Your patron teaches your familiar the secrets of transformation. Your familiar learns the *cursed metamorphosis* spell, even if it isn't on your tradition's spell list. You can spend 10 minutes to replace one prepared spell of 6th rank or higher with a *cursed metamorphosis* spell of the same rank.

---

## Level 16 Feats (2)

---

### Effortless Concentration :f:

**Feat 16** | **Bard, Druid, Sorcerer, Summoner, Witch, Wizard** *(Shared)*

**Source** Player Core pg. 106; Player Core 2 pg. 157

**Trigger** Your turn begins.

You can maintain a spell with hardly a thought. You immediately gain the effects of the Sustain action, allowing you to extend the duration of one of your active spells.

**Special** This can only be used on a cantrip or spell from the class matching the one you gained this feat from.

---

### Siphon Power :f:

**Feat 16** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

**Frequency** once per day

**Requirements** You haven't acted yet on your turn.

You draw upon your patron's power to cast a spell without expending your normal resources. You cast one spell your familiar knows from your patron or lessons without spending a spell slot or having it prepared. The spell must be at least 1 rank lower than your highest-rank spell slot.

**Special** This action gains the trait of your spell tradition.

---

## Level 18 Feats (2)

---

### Patron's Claim

**Feat 18** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

Your patron extends its influence through your familiar in a terrifying display of power. Your familiar gains the following activity.

**Patron's Claim** :aa: (concentrate)

**Frequency** once per hour

Your familiar's mouth opens unnaturally wide, and the limb of your patron stretches out to grasp at a creature within 30 feet. The target takes 10d10 spirit damage with a basic Fortitude save against your spell DC. On a failure, the target is also drained 2 (drained 4 on a critical failure), and you regain 1 Focus Point.

---

### Split Hex :a:

**Feat 18** | **Concentrate, Spellshape, Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

You split the power of your hex to affect another target. If the next action you use is to Cast a Spell that is a harmful hex with a single target, rather than targeting a single creature, you reduce the spell's rank by 2 and select a second target within range.

---

## Level 20 Feats (4)

---

### Demon's Hair

**Feat 20** | **Uncommon, Witch** | PFS Limited | ⚠️ *Legacy*

**Source** Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain pg. 75

**Prerequisites** Living Hair

Your hair constantly wriggles and writhes like that of your rival, Syu Tak-nwa. Your damage die of your hair unarmed Strike increases by one step and it gains the reach and versatile slashing traits. Your familiar assumes partial control over your hair; as long as your familiar is alive and within 30 feet, you are permanently quickened, and you can use the extra action only to make a hair Strike. You can deliver hexes through your hair. When you successfully cast a non-cantrip hex that requires 2 or more actions to cast and that doesn't require a spell attack roll, if your target is within your reach, as part of the spellcasting activity you can make a hair Strike against them before applying any effects of the hex. If this Strike misses, the hex has no effect.

**Special** If your hair already has the reach trait, it instead gains the sweep trait.

---

### Hex Master

**Feat 20** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

You embody the link to your patron so completely that you can cast your hexes in rapid succession. You are no longer limited to casting only a single hex each turn. In addition, when you cast *cackle* to sustain a hex, you gain the effects of Sustain for all your active hexes that have a duration of sustained.

---

### Patron's Truth

**Feat 20** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

**Prerequisites** patron's gift

You have mastered the greatest secrets of your patron's magic and learned a fundamental truth about your patron, even if their identity still remains a mystery. You gain an additional 10th-rank spell slot.

---

### Witch's Hut

**Feat 20** | **Witch**

**Source** Player Core pg. 191

You perform a 1-day rite to bind a hut, house, wagon, tent, or similar construction of up to Huge size to your will, transforming it into a living creature. The hut uses your AC, saving throws, and Perception modifier. It has 150 Hit Points, Hardness 10, and is immune to bleed, healing, the paralyzed condition, poison, and all conditions based on the mental trait. If the hut is destroyed, you can perform the rite again with the remains of the same structure or a new one. You can have only one active hut at a time.

You can command the hut using a single action that has the auditory and concentrate traits. You can issue the following commands:

- **Guard** The hut blocks entry through its doors and windows, attacking anyone who tries to break in with a strike of wood using your spell attack modifier.
- **Hide** The hut casts a 10th-rank illusion to disguise itself, using your spell DC.
- **Leap** The hut casts a 10th-rank teleport, using the destination you indicate. It can transport only itself and creatures currently inside it.
- **Lock** The hut casts *lock* on all its doors and windows.
- **Move** The hut sprouts legs and moves with a Speed of 60 feet. Its movement is too imprecise to be used in combat, but it can carry creatures inside it.

If you die, the hut continues following the last order you gave it.
